revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Inu Jinja]][[ja:伊奴神社]] {{Expand Japanese|伊奴神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Inu Shrine | native_name = 伊奴神社 | image = Inu Jinja 20130828.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Inu Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.208349|136.897676}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|ar|سوسانو|azb|سوسانو|bcl|Susanoo|be|Сусанаа|bg|Сусаноо|ca|Susanoo|cs|Susanoo|da|Susa-no-o|de|Susanoo|el|Σουσάνoο|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|eo|Susanoo|es|Susanoo|eu|Susanoo|fa|سوسانو (خدا)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|Սուսանո|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|ka|სუსანოო-ნო-მიკოტო|ko|스사노오|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|ꯁꯨꯁꯅꯨ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|Сусаноо|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|Сусаноо|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|சுசானவோ|th|ซูซาโนโอะ|tr|Susanoo|uk|Сусаноо|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|素戔嗚尊|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | website = {{Official website|https://inu-jinjya.or.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Inu Shrine'''|伊奴神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|ja|尾張国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 89 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039531|ltq=Q135039531|qid=Q135039531|1=Wokuchino Shrine (Q135039531)}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039533|ltq=Q135039533|qid=Q135039533|1=Kaneno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|lt=Nishi-ku|en|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|es|Nishi-ku (Nagoya)|fa|نیشی-کو، ناگویا|fr|Nishi-ku (Nagoya)|id|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|it|Nishi-ku (Nagoya)|ja|西区 (名古屋市)|ko|니시구 (나고야시)|pl|Nishi (Nagoja)|th|เขตนิชิ (นาโงยะ)|tl|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|tr|Nishi, Nagoya|uk|Район Нісі (Наґоя)|ur|نیشی وارڈ، ناگویا|zh|西區 (名古屋市)|qid=Q1207973|1=Nishi-ku, Nagoya}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Yamada district, Owari|lt=Yamada district, Owari|ja|山田郡 (尾張国)|zh|山田郡 (尾張國)|qid=Q11470699|1=Yamada district, Owari}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.208349|136.897676}} == official website (P856) == * https://inu-jinjya.or.jp/ == image (P18) == * Inu Jinja 20130828.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|ar|سوسانو|azb|سوسانو|bcl|Susanoo|be|Сусанаа|bg|Сусаноо|ca|Susanoo|cs|Susanoo|da|Susa-no-o|de|Susanoo|el|Σουσάνoο|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|eo|Susanoo|es|Susanoo|eu|Susanoo|fa|سوسانو (خدا)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|Սուսանո|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|ka|სუსანოო-ნო-მიკოტო|ko|스사노오|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|ꯁꯨꯁꯅꯨ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|Сусаноо|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|Сусаноо|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|சுசானவோ|th|ซูซาโนโอะ|tr|Susanoo|uk|Сусаноо|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|素戔嗚尊|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181429 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6180005000947 == Commons category (P373) == * Inu Jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市西区稲生町2-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Inu Jinja}} [[JA:伊奴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11379315}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Inu Shrine |Image = [[File:Inu Jinja (Nishi Ward, Nagoya) Vlux5 hdsr 21.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya City|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Nishi Ward (Nagoya City)|Q1207973|qid=Q1207973|lt=Nishi Ward|1=Nishi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Inō-chō (Nagoya City)|Q17193468|qid=Q17193468|lt=Inō-chō|1=Inō-chō}} 2-12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Toshigami|Q3028663|qid=Q3028663|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|1=Toshigami}}
Inuhime-no-Kami |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|qid=Q31105|1=673}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |Alternative names = Kumano Gongen
Jūnisho Gongen
Kumano Shrine
Kumano Myōjin
Inō Kumano Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second Sunday of October |Rituals = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Akamaro Ritual|1=UNKNOWN}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Inu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Inu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }}{{List of coordinates}} '''Inu Shrine''' (いぬじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya City|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Nishi Ward (Nagoya City)|Q1207973|qid=Q1207973|lt=Nishi Ward|1=Nishi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Inō-chō (Nagoya City)|Q17193468|qid=Q17193468|lt=Inō-chō|1=Inō-chō}}{{sfn|“Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names” Editorial Committee|1989|p=177}}{{sfn|Heibonsha Regional Materials Center, Ltd.|1993|p=127}}. It is identified with the “Inu Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Yamada District (Owari Province)|Q11470699|qid=Q11470699|lt=Yamada District|1=Yamada district, Owari}}. Because of its name “Inu” (dog), stone dog statues stand in front of the main hall, and the shrine is popular with people born in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Year of the Dog|1=UNKNOWN}} as well as dog lovers. In particular, on {{ill|lt=New Year’s Day|Q196627|qid=Q196627|1=New Year's Day}} in the Year of the Dog, large numbers of worshippers visit the shrine{{Cite news|title=“‘Dog’ shrine extremely busy: twice as many shrine maidens, ten times the amulets prepared”|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASKDC00BJKDBOIPE00G.html|publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company|date=2017-12-21|language=Japanese|accessdate=2018-01-01}}. === History === Up until the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}, it was referred to as **Kumano Gongen**. According to shrine tradition, in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}}), {{ill|lt=673|Q31105|qid=Q31105|1=673}}, when rice harvested in this area was presented to the imperial court, a shrine building was constructed on this site. It is said that twelve {{ill|lt=sacred mirrors|Q11591009|qid=Q11591009|1=Mirrors in Shinto}} have been handed down. In the surrounding area there is an {{ill|lt=archaeological site|Q839954|qid=Q839954|1=Archaeological site}} known as the “Inō Site,” where items such as {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|qid=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} dating from {{ill|lt=ancient times|Q41493|qid=Q41493|1=Ancient history}} through the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} have been excavated. The oldest {{ill|lt=ridge tag|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} is said to have dated from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|qid=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} ({{ill|lt=1430|Q6342|qid=Q6342|1=1430}}). A ridge tag from the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q8432|qid=Q8432|1=Civilization}} ({{ill|lt=1470|Q6569|qid=Q6569|1=1470}}) contains the following text{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1984|pp=1-2}}. {{Quotation|Dedicated construction of the hall of Amaterasu Ōmikami. Great donor: Minamoto no Ason, resident of Inō, Yamada, Owari Province. Shrine priest: Fujiwara Yasushige.|Bunmei 2 ridge tag}} Ridge tags from the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era list both “Kumano Myōjin” and “Inō Myōjin” side by side, and it has been suggested that in ancient times {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|qid=Q11568925|1=Kumano Gongen}} and {{ill|lt=Inari|Q1157265|qid=Q1157265|1=Dagmar Droysen-Reber}} were worshiped together{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1984|pp=1-2}}. {{Quotation|Reverently enshrining the secret Inō Myōjin: Ukanomitama no KamiWakumusubi no Kami, Uke Mochi no Kami, respectfully revised.|Genroku 8 ridge tag}} {{Quotation|Reverently enshrining the secret Kumano Myōjin: Ihe-nami no KamiHayatama no O no Kami, Amakumahito no Kami, Koto-saka no O no Kami, respectfully revised.|Genroku 8 ridge tag}} The early Edo-period document *{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|qid=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}* states, “Inō Village… Kumano Gongen, shrine precincts designated as tribute land”{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1984|pp=1-2}}. It is said that the Yamada family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|lt=end of the Edo period|Q804558|qid=Q804558|1=Bakumatsu}}{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1984|pp=1-2}}. In the mid-Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|qid=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified the shrine with “Inu Shrine” of Yamada District in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, and this view thereafter became the accepted theory. Because the location was Inō Village and the name resembled “Inu,” it appears that the shrine was identified as such. The *{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|qid=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}* records “Junior Third Rank Upper, Inu Tenjin”{{sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会|1989|p=177}}{{sfn|有限会社平凡社地方資料センター|1993|p=127}}. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|qid=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} and others have advanced the theory that the original Inu Shrine was actually the {{ill|lt=Tenjin|Q1305727|qid=Q1305727|1=Tenjin}} enshrined in the auxiliary sanctuary of this shrine. The *{{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|qid=Q137855962|1=Owarishi}}* suggests that Kumano Gongen was merely a {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q135009906|qid=Q135009906|1=Massha}} until the {{ill|lt=late Heian–Kamakura period|Q11395861|qid=Q11395861|1=late Heian–Kamakura period}}, but after it fell into ruin and was enshrined within the main sanctuary, it came to be treated as the principal deity. It also points out that place names and shrines with the same names as this shrine, such as “Inō-gō” and “Inu Shrine,” existed in {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|qid=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}} {{ill|lt=Aika District|Q11595889|qid=Q11595889|1=Aika district}}, as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|qid=Q11395858|1=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki}}*, suggesting a possible connection with this shrine. === Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|lt=祭神|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|lt=Principal deities|1=Saijin}}　 ** {{ill|lt=スサノオ|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) ** {{ill|lt=年神|Q3028663|qid=Q3028663|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|1=Toshigami}} (おおとしのかみ) ** Inuhime-no-Kami (いぬひめのかみ) * {{ill|lt=祭神|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|lt=Enshrined together|1=Saijin}} ** Wakumusubi-no-Kami {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} ** Ukanomitama-no-Kami {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} ** {{ill|lt=保食神|Q2280323|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|1=Ukemochi}} {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} ** {{ill|lt=イザナミ|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|1=Izanami}} {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} ** Hayatamao-no-Kami {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} ** Kotowakeo-no-Kami {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} ** {{ill|lt=天照大神|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} ** Kumano-no-Kami {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} === Shrines Within the Precincts === * Hakuryū Shrine ** There is a {{ill|lt=樹齢|Q11543638|qid=Q11543638|lt=Japanese chinquapin|1=tree age}} tree said to be 800 years old. * Tamanushi Inari Shrine {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} * Kasuga Shrine {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} * Tenjin Shrine * Hie Shrine (Hiyoshi Shrine) {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} * Ōsugi Shrine * Yaoyorozu Shrine {{sfn|西春日井郡|1923|p=317}} === Rituals === * {{ill|lt=左義長|Q9332740|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}}: Second Sunday of January * Tamanushi Inari Grand Festival: First Sunday of March {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.inu-jinjya.or.jp/event/tamanushi.html|title=玉主稲荷大祭|publisher=伊奴神社|language=日本語|accessdate=2018-01-01}} * Chinowa Ritual: Saturday and Sunday in late July {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.inu-jinjya.or.jp/event/shinzi.html|title=茅輪神事|publisher=伊奴神社|language=日本語|accessdate=2018-01-01}} * Annual Grand Festival: Second Sunday of October * Doll Memorial Service: First Sunday of December {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.inu-jinjya.or.jp/event/ningyou.html|title=人形供養祭|publisher=伊奴神社|language=日本語|accessdate=2018-01-01}} === Other === The shrine grounds were designated a “Nagoya City Green Space Conservation Area” on {{ill|lt=1980年|Q2439|qid=Q2439|lt=November 25, 1980|1=1980}} ({{ill|lt=昭和|Q327567|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 55) {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/ryokuseidoboku/cmsfiles/contents/0000016/16086/tokuryoku.siteijyoukyou.H29.3.pdf|page=1|format=PDF|title=特別緑地保全地区の指定状況 （平成29年3月1日現在）|author=名古屋市役所緑政土木局 緑地部 緑地維持課 緑化指導審査係|publisher=名古屋市|accessdate=2018-01-01|language=日本語}}, and is surrounded by many trees, including an 800-year-old {{ill|lt=シイ|Q1065400|qid=Q1065400|lt=Japanese chinquapin|1=Castanopsis}}. During the {{ill|lt=大相撲|Q10937013|qid=Q10937013|lt=Grand Sumo|1=Ōzumō}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=名古屋場所|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=伊勢ヶ濱部屋|Q11378930|qid=Q11378930|lt=Isegahama stable|1=Isegahama stable (2007)}} uses the shrine as lodging, and sumo wrestlers can be seen practicing on the {{ill|lt=土俵|Q832429|qid=Q832429|lt=dohyō|1=Dohyō}} within the precincts. The shrine is known for prayers for safe childbirth, fertility, marital harmony, and household safety. After a safe childbirth prayer, worshippers offer prayers in front of the dog statues within the precincts. === Access === * {{ill|lt=名古屋市営地下鉄|Q1144874|qid=Q1144874|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}} {{ill|lt=名古屋市営地下鉄鶴舞線|Q1043806|qid=Q1043806|lt=Tsurumai Line|1=Tsurumai Line}}: Get off at {{ill|lt=庄内通駅|Q863241|qid=Q863241|lt=Shōnai-dōri Station|1=Shōnai-dōri Station}} . * {{ill|lt=名古屋市営バス|Q11414982|qid=Q11414982|lt=Nagoya City Bus|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}}: Get off at “Eko” bus stop on routes Meieki 13, Sakae 11, or Sakae 13, or at “Inō-chō” bus stop on route Sakae 13 . === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.inu-jinjya.or.jp/event/reitaisai.html|title=例大祭|publisher=伊奴神社|language=日本語|accessdate=2018-01-01}} {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.inu-jinjya.or.jp/yuisho/index.html|title=伊奴神社の御由緒|language=日本語|publisher=伊奴神社|accessdate=2018-01-01}} {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.inu-jinjya.or.jp/access/index.html|title=アクセスマップ|language=日本語|publisher=伊奴神社|accessdate=2018-01-01}} |2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=西春日井郡|title=西春日井郡誌|publisher=西春日井郡|year=1923|date=1923-3|language=日本語|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=山田寂雀|author2=西岡寿一|title=西区の歴史|publisher=愛知県郷土資料刊行会|year=1983|isbn=4-87161-011-X|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会|year=1989|date=1989-03-08|title={{ill|lt=角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011|qid=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 23 Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=角川書店|Q1054765|qid=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}|isbn=4-04-001230-5|language=日本語|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=有限会社平凡社地方資料センター|title=Place Names of Aichi Prefecture {{ill|lt=日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}}
